channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CBI apprehends key suspect in 2026 NEET-UG chemistry paper leak
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has apprehended PV Kulkarni, identified as the main accused in the 2026 NEET-UG paper leak. Kulkarni, a chemistry lecturer from Latur, Maharashtra, was involv...

item.title: GetUp! calls for Australian Press Council reform in new campaign
item.description: Progressive advocacy group GetUp! has rolled out a new campaign aimed at reforming the Australian Press Council. The initiative marks the organisation's first major campaign since a tumultuous 2025. A...

item.title: Kerala SSLC Results Announced by Former Rank Holder Principal Secretary Sharmila Mary Joseph
item.description: In a heartwarming coincidence, Sharmila Mary Joseph, Principal Secretary of the General Education Department in Kerala, announced the SSLC examination results on May 15, 2026. She herself was a topper...

item.title: Canadian Web Series ‘Pioneer Girl’ Brings Lesser-Known Women in History to Light
item.description: The new Canadian web series Pioneer Girl, starring Kat Barrell of Wynonna Earp, offers a spirited and humorous take on Canadian history through the lens of women’s contributions, often overlooked in t...

item.title: Tanzania and Russia Advance Strategic Cooperation in Trade, Energy and Mining
item.description: Tanzania is working closely with Russia to strengthen their bilateral relations to a strategic level. On 9th February 2026, Tanzania’s Minister of Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation, Ambassa...

item.title: Punjab government allows extended business hours until June 1
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab government has announced that it is easing restrictions on business operating hours across the province until 1st June. Effective immediately, markets, shopping malls, hotels, resta...

item.title: Canadians and Americans buying less alcohol due to affordability and lifestyle changes
item.description: Recent data from Statistics Canada and industry sources reveal a notable decline in beer and alcohol sales across Canada, mirroring trends in the United States. Retail alcohol sales in Canada fell 3.4...

item.title: Supreme Court Denies Request to Protect Draft Senate Flood Control Report
item.description: The Supreme Court has denied a petition seeking a temporary restraining order (TRO) to prevent the Senate and its Blue Ribbon Committee from modifying or discarding drafts of the report on the flood c...

item.title: Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrial on rape charge ends in mistrial due to deadlocked jury
item.description: Harvey Weinstein’s third trial in New York on rape allegations has ended in a mistrial after the jury could not reach a unanimous verdict. The case centred on claims by aspiring actress Jessica Mann t...

item.title: Five Italian tourists die in cave diving accident in the Maldives
item.description: Five Italian tourists, including a university professor and her 20-year-old daughter, tragically lost their lives in a scuba diving accident while exploring underwater caves in Vaavu Atoll, Maldives. ...

item.title: Bayelsa Governor Diri Advises Against Inaugurating New INC Leadership Amid Ongoing Litigation
item.description: Bayelsa State Governor, Senator Douye Diri, don warn the outgoing Prof. Benjamin Okaba-led executive of the Ijaw National Congress (INC) and their Electoral Committee make dem no go ahead with the pla...

item.title: The View Criticizes Trump's Inclusion of Brett Ratner in China Delegation Amid Epstein Files
item.description: Hosts of ABC's 'The View' expressed strong disapproval of President Donald Trump's decision to include filmmaker Brett Ratner in the official U.S. delegation accompanying him to China. The criticism c...

item.title: Meningitis treatment given to students at two schools after reported pupil death
item.description: Authorities have reported that two students attending different schools have received treatment for meningitis after the death of a pupil. The incident has raised concern among parents, teachers, and ...

item.title: Deputy President's Office Seeks Extra Funds for Gratuity as Staff Plan to Exit for 2027 Politics
item.description: A significant number of employees at the Office of the Deputy President are planning to leave public service ahead of the 2027 General Election. This development has forced the office to request an ad...

item.title: Jalang'o and Amina Chao Attend Dr Ofweneke’s Wedding and Entertain Guests with Dance
item.description: On Saturday, 9th May, TV host and comedian Bush Sande Oyolo, famously known as Dr Ofweneke, tied the knot with influencer Diana Ingosi in a vibrant traditional Luhya wedding held in Kakamega County. T...

item.title: UK A&E doctors and nurses praise The Pitt for its realistic depiction of emergency medicine
item.description: The HBO drama series The Pitt has received widespread praise from UK A&E doctors and nurses for its authentic portrayal of life in an emergency department. Clinicians highlight how the show accurately...

item.title: Analysing the Chande Commission Report on October 2025 Events and Generational Disconnect in Tanzania
item.description: The article by Anna Bwana discusses the Chande Commission report handed to President Samia after the October 29, 2025 violence during elections in Tanzania. The writer, who works in democracy and deve...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Sees Strong Growth in Week 8 of 2026, MCB Leads Gains
item.description: During Week 8 of 2026, the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) showed strong market activity with both equity and bond trading increasing significantly. Equity turnover jumped by 49.80% to TZS 87.80 bi...

item.title: Senator Bong Go urges completion and full equipping of Super Health Centers amid reports of missing utilities
item.description: Senator Bong Go, vice chairman of the Senate committee on health, expressed serious concern over the incomplete status of several Super Health Centers across the country. During a recent committee hea...

item.title: GSM to Build Modern 20,000-Seat Stadium for Yanga
item.description: The CEO of GSM Group, Aisha Mohamed, has announced plans for a new 20,000-seater stadium for Young Africans (Yanga) football club in Dar es Salaam. She described the project as more than just a footba...
